import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels_1d: int,
        out_channels_1d: int,
        kernel_size_1d: int,
        in_channels_3d: int,
        out_channels_3d: int,
        kernel_size_3d: tuple,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.weight_1d = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(out_channels_1d, in_channels_1d, kernel_size_1d))
        self.conv3d = nn.Conv3d(in_channels_3d, out_channels_3d, kernel_size_3d)

    def forward(self, x1d: torch.Tensor, x3d: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x1d = F.conv1d(x1d, self.weight_1d)
        x3d = self.conv3d(x3d)
        return x1d, x3d

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, split_size, num_segments):
        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.num_segments = num_segments
        self.identity = nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.split(x, split_size_or_sections=self.split_size, dim=1)
        x = [self.identity(segment) for segment in x]
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, ceil_value, linspace_start, linspace_end, linspace_steps):
        super().__init__()
        self.ceil_value = ceil_value
        self.linspace_start = linspace_start
        self.linspace_end = linspace_end
        self.linspace_steps = linspace_steps

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.ceil(x * self.ceil_value)  # Apply ceil operation
        x = torch.linspace(
            self.linspace_start, self.linspace_end, steps=self.linspace_steps, out=x
        )  # Apply linspace operation
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.tensor_size = tensor_size

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, eps_val: float):
        super().__init__()
        self.eps_val = eps_val

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply torch.nonzero
        non_zero_indices = torch.nonzero(x)
        # Apply F.normalize
        normalized_x = F.normalize(x, p=2, dim=0, eps=self.eps_val)
        return normalized_x

# Define the model
class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_features, eps=1e-05, affine=True):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.max = torch.nn.MaxPool2d(2)
        self.group_norm = nn.GroupNorm(num_groups=32, num_channels=input_features, eps=eps, affine=affine)
        self.avg_pool = nn.AvgPool2d(2)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.max(x)
        x = self.group_norm(x)
        x = self.avg_pool(x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, split_size, num_splits):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.num_splits = num_splits

    def forward(self, x):
        # Split tensor along dimension 1
        split_tensors = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=1)
        # Concatenate the split tensors
        concatenated = torch.cat(split_tensors, dim=1)
        return concatenated

        super().__init__()
        self.group_norm = nn.GroupNorm(num_groups, num_channels)

        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_shape = input_shape
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # all operation
        x_all = torch.all(x, dim=self.dim)
        # any operation
        x_any = torch.any(x, dim=self.dim)
        return x_all, x_any

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings, embedding_dim, gather_indices_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim)
        self.gather_indices_dim = gather_indices_dim

    def forward(self, input_indices, gather_indices):
        embedded = self.embedding(input_indices)
        gathered = torch.gather(embedded, self.gather_indices_dim, gather_indices)
        return gathered

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_shape, reduction):
        super().__init__()
        self.tensor1 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(tensor_shape))
        self.tensor2 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(tensor_shape))
        self.reduction = reduction

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        x1 = x1.view(self.tensor1.shape)
        x2 = x2.view(self.tensor2.shape)
        x = F.kl_div(x1, x2, reduction=self.reduction)
        x = torch.all(x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, k_value, divisor):
        super().__init__()
        self.k_value = k_value
        self.divisor = divisor

    def forward(self, x):

        x = torch.div(x, self.divisor)
        return x, top_indices

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int, parameter_dict: dict):
        super().__init__()
        self.parameter_dict = nn.ParameterDict(parameter_dict)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        triu_indices = torch.triu(x).nonzero(as_tuple=True)
        triu_elements = x[triu_indices]
        modified_elements = self.parameter_dict["modified_elements"] * triu_elements
        x[triu_indices] = modified_elements
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, min_value, max_value):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.min_value = min_value
        self.max_value = max_value

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.min_value, max=self.max_value)
        x = torch.minimum(x, torch.tensor(self.max_value))
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, kernel_size, stride, avg_pool_kernel_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.cumsum = torch.cumsum
        self.avg_pool = nn.AvgPool1d(kernel_size=avg_pool_kernel_size, stride=stride)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.cumsum(x, dim=0)
        x = self.avg_pool(x.transpose(1, 2)).transpose(1, 2)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        is_complex = torch.is_complex(x)
        condition = x > self.threshold
        x = torch.where(condition, x, torch.zeros_like(x))
        return x, is_complex

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features1, out_features1, in_features2, out_features2):
        super().__init__()
        self.module_dict = nn.ModuleDict(
            {"linear1": nn.Linear(in_features1, out_features1), "linear2": nn.Linear(in_features2, out_features2)}
        )

    def forward(self, x1, x2, y1, y2):
        out1 = self.module_dict["linear1"](x1)
        out2 = self.module_dict["linear2"](x2)
        equal1 = torch.equal(out1, y1)
        equal2 = torch.equal(out2, y2)
        return equal1, equal2

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, num_channels, height, width):
        super().__init__()
        self.bn = nn.BatchNorm2d(num_features)
        self.num_channels = num_channels
        self.height = height
        self.width = width

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.transpose(
            x, 1, 2
        )  # Transpose from (batch, channels, height, width) to (batch, height, channels, width)
        x = torch.transpose(
            x, 2, 3
        )  # Transpose from (batch, height, channels, width) to (batch, height, width, channels)
        x = torch.transpose(x, 2, 3)  # Transpose back to (batch, height, channels, width)
        x = torch.transpose(x, 1, 2)  # Transpose back to (batch, channels, height, width)
        x = self.bn(x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, num_features, eps=1e-8):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.eps = eps

    def forward(self, x, y):
        and_result = torch.logical_and(x, y)
        cosine_similarity = F.cosine_similarity(
            x.view(self.batch_size, self.num_features), y.view(self.batch_size, self.num_features), dim=1, eps=self.eps
        )
        return and_result, cosine_similarity

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use torch.gt to create a mask where x is greater than threshold
        mask = torch.gt(x, self.threshold)
        # Use the mask to clone x
        x_cloned = torch.clone(x)
        # Apply the mask to x_cloned
        x_cloned[mask] = 0
        return x_cloned

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size, output_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(input_size, hidden_size)
        self.output = nn.Linear(hidden_size, output_size)

    def forward(self, x, y):
        # Compute cosine similarity
        sim_score = F.cosine_similarity(x, y)
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = F.relu(x)
        x = self.output(x)
        return sim_score, x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, divisor, split_sizes, num_splits):
        super().__init__()
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.split_sizes = split_sizes
        self.num_splits = num_splits

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.div(x, self.divisor)
        x = torch.split_with_sizes(x, self.split_sizes, dim=0)
        x = torch.cat(x[: self.num_splits], dim=0)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, start, end, steps, kernel_size, dilation, padding, stride):
        super().__init__()
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.steps = steps
        self.kernel_size = kernel_size
        self.dilation = dilation
        self.padding = padding
        self.stride = stride

    def forward(self, x):
        linearspace = torch.linspace(self.start, self.end, self.steps)
        unfolded = F.unfold(
            x, kernel_size=self.kernel_size, dilation=self.dilation, padding=self.padding, stride=self.stride
        )
        return unfolded

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_channels, num_classes, loss_reduction="mean"):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_channels = input_channels
        self.num_classes = num_classes
        self.loss_reduction = loss_reduction

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        x = torch.cat((x1, x2), dim=1)
        loss = F.smooth_l1_loss(x, torch.zeros_like(x), reduction=self.loss_reduction)
        return loss

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.einsum_expression = einsum_expression
        self.normalize_p = normalize_p
        self.normalize_dim = normalize_dim

        x = F.normalize(x, p=self.normalize_p, dim=self.normalize_dim)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, reduction):
        super().__init__()
        self.kl_div_loss = nn.KLDivLoss(reduction=reduction)
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, input, target):
        max_values, _ = torch.max(input, self.dim)
        loss = self.kl_div_loss(input, target)
        return max_values, loss

        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim, padding_idx=pad_idx)

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, interpolate_scale_factor, interpolate_mode, module_dict_keys, module_dict_values):
        super().__init__()
        self.interpolate_scale_factor = interpolate_scale_factor
        self.interpolate_mode = interpolate_mode
        self.module_dict = nn.ModuleDict(dict(zip(module_dict_keys, module_dict_values)))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.interpolate(x, scale_factor=self.interpolate_scale_factor, mode=self.interpolate_mode)
        for key in self.module_dict.keys():
            x = self.module_dict[key](x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold1, threshold2):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold1 = threshold1
        self.threshold2 = threshold2

    def forward(self, x):
        ge_mask = torch.ge(x, self.threshold1)
        lt_mask = torch.lt(x, self.threshold2)
        x = x * ge_mask * lt_mask
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Ensure the input tensor is within the valid domain for acos
        x = torch.clamp(x, -1.0, 1.0)
        # Apply acos operation
        x = torch.acos(x)
        # Convert from degrees to radians
        x = torch.deg2rad(x)
        return x

        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.fc = nn.Linear(input_size, output_size)

        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.num_features = num_features

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_height, input_width, target_height, target_width):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_height = input_height
        self.input_width = input_width
        self.target_height = target_height
        self.target_width = target_width

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x_coords, y_coords = torch.meshgrid(
            torch.arange(self.input_height), torch.arange(self.input_width), indexing="ij"
        )
        y_coords, x_coords = torch.meshgrid(
            torch.arange(self.target_height), torch.arange(self.target_width), indexing="ij"
        )
        loss = F.smooth_l1_loss(x[:, :, x_coords, y_coords], y[:, :, y_coords, x_coords])
        return loss

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size, batch_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Calculate standard deviation along the last dimension
        std = torch.std(x, dim=1)
        # Matrix multiplication with a fixed matrix
        mm = torch.mm(std.view(self.batch_size, 1), std.view(self.batch_size, 1).t())
        return mm

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_shape):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.tensor = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*tensor_shape))

    def forward(self, k):
        # Check if tensor is negative infinity using torch.isneginf
        isneginf_result = torch.isneginf(self.tensor)

        # Get top-k largest elements and their indices using torch.topk
        topk_result, topk_indices = torch.topk(self.tensor, k)

        return isneginf_result, topk_result, topk_indices

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, comparison_value, cos_scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.comparison_value = comparison_value
        self.cos_scaling_factor = cos_scaling_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.gt(x, self.comparison_value)  # Apply torch.gt
        x = x.float()  # Convert the boolean mask to float
        x = torch.cos(x * self.cos_scaling_factor)  # Apply torch.cos

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        # No Need for Initialization as operations doesn't require parameters

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply torch.isinf to check for inf values in x
        x_inf = torch.isinf(x)

        # Apply torch.masked_select to select elements from x according to the mask
        selected = torch.masked_select(x, mask)
        return x_inf, selected

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_channels, split_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Split the input tensor along the channel dimension
        split_tensors = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=1)

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dividend_shape, divisor_shape, exponent1, exponent2):
        super().__init__()
        self.dividend = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(dividend_shape))
        self.divisor = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(divisor_shape))
        self.exponent1 = exponent1
        self.exponent2 = exponent2

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.remainder(x + self.dividend, 1)
        x = torch.pow(x, self.exponent1)
        x = torch.remainder(x, 1)
        x = torch.pow(x, self.exponent2)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, k_top):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.k_top = k_top

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply topk operation
        top_values, top_indices = torch.topk(x, k=self.k_top)
        # Apply triu operation
        triu_indices = torch.triu_indices(row=self.input_size, col=self.input_size, offset=0)
        triu_values = x[triu_indices[0], triu_indices[1]]
        return top_values, triu_values

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels,
        out_channels_transpose,
        out_channels_conv,
        kernel_size_transpose,
        kernel_size_conv,
        stride,
        padding,
        bias_shape,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose1d(
            in_channels, out_channels_transpose, kernel_size_transpose, stride=stride, padding=padding
        )
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(out_channels_transpose, out_channels_conv, kernel_size_conv, padding=padding)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv_transpose(x)
        x = self.conv(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

        self.clamp_max = clamp_max
        self.tril_dim1 = tril_dim1

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features):
        super().__init__()
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(in_features, out_features))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use torch.unbind to split the input tensor into a sequence of tensors along dimension 0
        x_unbind = torch.unbind(x, dim=0)
        # Use torch.prod to compute the product of each tensor in the sequence
        product_tensor = torch.prod(torch.stack(x_unbind), dim=0)
        return product_tensor

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold, squared_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.squared_value = squared_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.gt to x with threshold
        x = torch.gt(x, self.threshold)
        # Apply torch.square to x
        x = torch.square(x.float())
        # Multiply with squared_value
        x = x * self.squared_value
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim: int, mesh_dims: tuple, nan_to_num_vals: tuple):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_dim = input_dim
        self.mesh_dims = mesh_dims
        self.nan_to_num_vals = nan_to_num_vals

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Create meshgrid
        grid_x, grid_y = torch.meshgrid([torch.arange(self.input_dim), torch.arange(self.input_dim)])
        x[..., grid_x, grid_y] = torch.nan_to_num(
            x[..., grid_x, grid_y],
            nan=self.nan_to_num_vals[0],
            posinf=self.nan_to_num_vals[1],
            neginf=self.nan_to_num_vals[2],
        )
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, split_size, num_splits, min_value, max_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.num_splits = num_splits
        self.min_value = min_value
        self.max_value = max_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # split the input tensor into chunks
        x = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=self.input_size)
        # apply clamp operation to each chunk
        x = [torch.clamp(chunk, min=self.min_value, max=self.max_value) for chunk in x]
        # concatenate the chunks back together
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=self.input_size)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, eps=1e-5):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_norm = nn.BatchNorm3d(num_features)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.einsum("bixyz, icxyz -> bixyz", x, self.batch_norm.weight.view(1, -1, 1, 1, 1))
        x = x + self.batch_norm.bias.view(1, -1, 1, 1, 1)
        x = torch.einsum("bixyz, icxyz -> bixyz", x, self.batch_norm.running_mean.view(1, -1, 1, 1, 1))
        x = x / torch.sqrt(self.batch_norm.running_var.view(1, -1, 1, 1, 1) + self.batch_norm.eps)
        x = torch.einsum("bixyz, icxyz -> bixyz", x, self.batch_norm.weight.view(1, -1, 1, 1, 1))
        x = x + self.batch_norm.bias.view(1, -1, 1, 1, 1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, interpolate_scale_factor, mode="bilinear", align_corners=False):
        super().__init__()
        self.interpolate_scale_factor = interpolate_scale_factor
        self.mode = mode
        self.align_corners = align_corners

    def forward(self, x):
        # Sort the input tensor along the last dimension
        _, sorted_indices = torch.sort(x, dim=-1)
        sorted_x = torch.gather(x, dim=-1, index=sorted_indices)

        # Apply F.interpolate to the sorted tensor
        interpolated_x = F.interpolate(
            sorted_x, scale_factor=self.interpolate_scale_factor, mode=self.mode, align_corners=self.align_corners
        )
        return interpolated_x

        similarity = F.cosine_similarity(A, B, self.dim)
        selected_similarity = torch.index_select(similarity, self.similarity_dim, index)
        return selected_similarity

# The logical_or and polar operations do not fit into a standard neural network architecture and do not support parameters like conv2d, convTranspose2d, etc.
# Consequently, we have to use a different approach. Here is an example where the logical_or operation is used on tensors created by the forward method.


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, polar_input1, polar_input2):
        super().__init__()
        self.polar_input1 = polar_input1
        self.polar_input2 = polar_input2

    def forward(self, x, y):
        polar_x = torch.polar(x, y)
        polar_y = torch.polar(y, x)
        logical_or = torch.logical_or(polar_x, polar_y)
        return logical_or

        super().__init__()
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply sinusoidal transformation to the input tensor
        x = torch.sin(x)

        # Reshape the tensor to a suitable form for cosine similarity
        x = x.view(x.size(0), self.input_size, -1)

        # Calculate cosine similarity
        cos_sim = F.cosine_similarity(x.permute(0, 2, 1), x.permute(0, 2, 1), dim=2)

        return cos_sim

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose1d(
            in_channels,
            out_channels,
            kernel_size,
            stride=stride,
            padding=padding,
            output_padding=output_padding,
            bias=bias,
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.argmax(x, dim=-1, keepdim=True).float()
        x = F.conv_transpose1d(
            x,
            self.conv_transpose.weight,
            self.conv_transpose.bias,
            stride=self.conv_transpose.stride,
            padding=self.conv_transpose.padding,
            output_padding=self.conv_transpose.output_padding,
        )
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, width: int, height: int, val1: int, val2: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.val1 = val1
        self.val2 = val2
        self.width = width
        self.height = height

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Create a boolean mask for values greater than or equal to val1
        mask1 = torch.eq(x, self.val1)
        # Create another mask for values greater than or equal to val2
        mask2 = torch.eq(x, self.val2)
        # Use torch.logical_or to combine the masks
        combined_mask = torch.logical_or(mask1, mask2)
        # Apply the mask to the input tensor
        x[combined_mask] = self.val1
        x[~combined_mask] = self.val2
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply acos to each element in the input tensor
        x = torch.acos(torch.clamp(x, -1.0, 1.0))
        # Apply exp to each element in the input tensor after adding the bias

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, multiplier: float):
        super().__init__()
        self.multiplier = multiplier

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Flatten the tensor for non-zero operation
        flat_tensor = x.view(-1)
        # Get the indices of non-zero elements
        nonzero_indices = torch.nonzero(flat_tensor).squeeze()
        # Multiply the tensor by a scalar
        multiplied_tensor = torch.multiply(flat_tensor, self.multiplier)
        # Re-arrange the multiplied tensor using the non-zero indices
        result_tensor = torch.zeros_like(flat_tensor)
        result_tensor[nonzero_indices] = multiplied_tensor[nonzero_indices]
        # Reshape the tensor back to original shape
        result_tensor = result_tensor.view_as(x)
        return result_tensor

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, dist_p, tanh_beta):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_shape = input_shape
        self.dist_p = dist_p
        self.tanh_beta = tanh_beta

    def forward(self, x):
        if x.shape != self.input_shape:
            raise ValueError("Input shape does not match the expected shape.")
        dist = torch.dist(x, torch.zeros_like(x), p=self.dist_p)
        tanh = torch.tanh(dist * self.tanh_beta)
        return tanh

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, split_size, feature_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.feature_size = feature_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # split the tensor along dimension 0
        x_split = torch.split(x, self.split_size)
        # apply erf function to each split part
        x_split_erf = [torch.erf(i) for i in x_split]
        # concatenate the parts along dimension 0

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, reduction: str = "mean"):
        super().__init__()
        self.kl_div_loss = nn.KLDivLoss(reduction=reduction)

    def forward(self, log_preds: torch.Tensor, targets: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        log_preds = torch.neg(log_preds)  # Replace with torch.neg for exact reverse of exp operation in KLDivLoss
        loss = self.kl_div_loss(log_preds, targets)
        return loss

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, split_size_or_sections, modulus):
        super().__init__()
        self.split_size_or_sections = split_size_or_sections
        self.modulus = modulus

    def forward(self, x):
        # Assuming x is a 3D tensor for simplicity
        x = torch.split(x, self.split_size_or_sections, dim=1)
        x = [torch.fmod(t, self.modulus) for t in x]
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=1)
        return x

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels_3d,
        out_channels_3d,
        kernel_size_3d,
        stride_3d,
        padding_3d,
        in_channels_1d,
        out_channels_1d,
        kernel_size_1d,
        stride_1d,
        padding_1d,
        output_padding_1d,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv3d = nn.Conv3d(in_channels_3d, out_channels_3d, kernel_size_3d, stride=stride_3d, padding=padding_3d)
        self.conv_transpose1d = nn.ConvTranspose1d(
            in_channels_1d,
            out_channels_1d,
            kernel_size_1d,
            stride=stride_1d,
            padding=padding_1d,
            output_padding=output_padding_1d,
        )

    def forward(self, x3d, x1d):
        x3d = self.conv3d(x3d)
        x1d = self.conv_transpose1d(x1d)
        return x3d, x1d

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
        self.batch_size = batch_size

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, clamp_min, clamp_max, log10_min, log10_max):
        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min
        self.clamp_max = clamp_max
        self.log10_min = log10_min
        self.log10_max = log10_max

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.clamp_min, max=self.clamp_max)
        x = torch.log10(x)
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.log10_min, max=self.log10_max)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, m_shape, mat1_shape, mat2_shape, shifts, dims):
        super().__init__()
        self.m = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(m_shape))
        self.mat1 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(mat1_shape))
        self.mat2 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(mat2_shape))
        self.shifts = shifts
        self.dims = dims

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.addmm(self.m, self.mat1, self.mat2)
        x = torch.roll(x, self.shifts, self.dims)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, upscale_factor, channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.pixel_shuffle = nn.PixelShuffle(upscale_factor)

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        similarity = F.cosine_similarity(x1, x2, dim=1, eps=1e-6)
        x1 = self.pixel_shuffle(x1)
        x2 = self.pixel_shuffle(x2)
        return similarity, x1, x2

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, condition_tensor, positive_tensor, negative_tensor):
        super().__init__()
        self.param_dict = nn.ParameterDict()
        self.param_dict.update({"positive": nn.Parameter(positive_tensor)})
        self.param_dict.update({"negative": nn.Parameter(negative_tensor)})
        self.condition = nn.Parameter(condition_tensor, requires_grad=False)

    def forward(self, x):
        positive = self.param_dict["positive"]
        negative = self.param_dict["negative"]
        condition_tensor = self.condition
        return torch.where(condition_tensor > 0.5, positive, negative)

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, dim1, dim2):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.dim1 = torch.nn.Linear(input_dim, dim1)
        self.dim2 = torch.nn.Linear(dim1, dim2)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.eq(x, x)  # elementwise equality comparison
        x = x.to(torch.float)  # convert boolean tensor to float tensor
        x = self.dim1(x)
        x = self.dim2(x)
        x = torch.mm(x, x.t())  # matrix multiplication
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, comparison_value):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.comparison_value = comparison_value

    def forward(self, x):
        comparison = torch.full(self.input_size, self.comparison_value, device=x.device)
        greater_equal = torch.ge(x, comparison)
        minimum = torch.minimum(x, comparison)
        return greater_equal, minimum

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, inplace: bool, negative_slope: float, mask_value: float):
        super().__init__()
        self.leaky_relu = nn.LeakyReLU(negative_slope=negative_slope, inplace=inplace)
        self.mask_value = mask_value

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.leaky_relu(x)
        mask = torch.tensor(self.mask_value, device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype)
        x = torch.masked_fill(x, x >= mask, mask)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, max_threshold, any_threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_threshold = max_threshold
        self.any_threshold = any_threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.max to x with max_threshold
        max_result, _ = torch.max(x, dim=1, keepdim=True)
        max_mask = max_result > self.max_threshold

        # Apply torch.any to x with any_threshold
        any_mask = torch.any(x > self.any_threshold, dim=1, keepdim=True)

        # Combine the two masks
        combined_mask = torch.logical_and(max_mask, any_mask)

        # Apply the combined mask to x
        result = torch.where(combined_mask, x, torch.zeros_like(x))
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Reshape input to have the second dimension match the input size
        x = x.view(-1, self.input_size)

        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, k: int, min_val: float):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.k = k
        self.min_val = min_val

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply topk operation
        topk_values, topk_indices = torch.topk(x, self.k)
        # Apply clamp_min operation
        clamped_values = torch.clamp_min(topk_values, self.min_val)
        return clamped_values

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_size, out_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.in_size = in_size
        self.out_size = out_size

    def forward(self, x, y):
        z1, z2 = torch.meshgrid(x, y, indexing="ij")
        z = torch.stack((z1, z2), 2)
        z = z.view(-1, 2)
        z = torch.sigmoid(z)
        return z

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, input_size, other_size, bias_shape):
        super(Model, self).__init__()

        self.input_size = input_size
        self.other_size = other_size
        self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x, other):
        x = x.view(self.batch_size, self.input_size, 1)

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use torch.nonzero to find the indices of non-zero elements
        nonzero_indices = torch.nonzero(x)

        # Use torch.ge to compare the tensor elements with the threshold
        greater_than_threshold = torch.ge(x, self.threshold)

        # Combine the operations
        combined_tensor = torch.where(greater_than_threshold, x, torch.zeros_like(x))
        return combined_tensor, nonzero_indices

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.cos_layer = nn.CosineSimilarity()

    def forward(self, x1: torch.Tensor, x2: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Compute cosine similarity
        cos_sim = self.cos_layer(x1, x2)
        # Apply torch.cos to the result
        cos_sim = torch.cos(cos_sim)
        # Compute MSE loss
        mse_loss = F.mse_loss(x1, x2)
        # Combine the results
        result = cos_sim - mse_loss
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x, target):
        split_size = self.input_size // 2
        x1, x2 = torch.split(x, split_size, dim=1)
        loss1 = F.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits(x1, target[:, :split_size])
        loss2 = F.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits(x2, target[:, split_size:])
        total_loss = loss1 + loss2
        return total_loss

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, min_val, where_threshold):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_dim = input_dim
        self.min_val = min_val
        self.where_threshold = where_threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # applying torch.min
        min_op = torch.min(x, dim=self.input_dim, keepdim=True)
        min_result = min_op.values

        # applying torch.where
        where_condition = min_result < self.where_threshold
        where_result = torch.where(where_condition, min_result, x)

        return where_result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_shape, logits, temperature):
        super().__init__()
        self.tensor = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(tensor_shape))
        self.logits = logits
        self.temperature = temperature

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.log_softmax(x / self.temperature, dim=self.logits)
        x = torch.logical_and(x, self.tensor)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, embed_dim, reduction="mean"):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
        self.reduction = reduction

    def forward(self, x, y):
        similarity = F.cosine_similarity(x, y, dim=1, eps=1e-8)
        loss = F.smooth_l1_loss(similarity, torch.zeros_like(similarity), reduction=self.reduction)
        return loss

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, num_classes, on_value, off_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.num_classes = num_classes
        self.on_value = on_value
        self.off_value = off_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.tril to the input tensor
        tril_x = torch.tril(x, diagonal=0)
        # Apply F.one_hot to the resulting tensor
        one_hot = F.one_hot(tril_x.long(), num_classes=self.num_classes).float()
        one_hot *= (one_hot == 1).float()
        one_hot += (one_hot == 0).float() * self.off_value
        one_hot += (one_hot > 0).float() * (one_hot < 1).float() * self.on_value
        return one_hot

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, repeats, output_features, beta, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.softplus = nn.Softplus(beta=beta, threshold=threshold)
        self.repeats = repeats
        self.linear = nn.Linear(output_features // repeats, output_features // repeats)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, self.repeats, dim=1)
        x = self.softplus(x)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, min_val, max_val):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.min_val = min_val
        self.max_val = max_val

    def forward(self, x, y):
        z = torch.sub(x, y)  # Subtract y from x
        mask = torch.lt(z, self.min_val)  # Create a mask where z < min_val
        z = torch.where(
            mask, torch.full_like(z, self.max_val), z
        )  # Replace values in z where mask is True with max_val
        return z

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_shape):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.tensor_shape = tensor_shape

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.triu to the input tensor
        triu_x = torch.triu(x, diagonal=0)
        # Create a boolean mask using torch.triu
        mask = torch.logical_and(triu_x >= 0, triu_x <= 1)
        # Apply torch.logical_and with the mask
        result = torch.logical_and(triu_x, mask)
        return result

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias_shape, scaling_factor
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))
        self.scaling_factor = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(scaling_factor))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv_transpose(x)
        x = torch.nn.functional.threshold(
            x, 1, 1
        )  # torch.nn.functional.threshold is equivalent to torch.clamp in this case.
        x = x * self.scaling_factor
        x = torch.nn.functional.threshold(
            x, 1, 1
        )  # torch.nn.functional.threshold is equivalent to torch.clamp in this case.
        x = x / self.scaling_factor
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, shape_a, shape_b):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.tensor_a = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(shape_a))
        self.tensor_b = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(shape_b))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Applying torch.isinf to tensor_a and tensor_b
        inf_tensor_a = torch.isinf(self.tensor_a)
        inf_tensor_b = torch.isinf(self.tensor_b)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, clamp_min, clamp_max, sign_threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min
        self.clamp_max = clamp_max
        self.sign_threshold = sign_threshold

    def forward(self, x):

        x = torch.sign(x - self.sign_threshold)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, output_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.relu = nn.ReLU()
        self.argmax = input_dim
        self.output_dim = output_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.relu(x)
        x = torch.argmax(x, dim=1)
        x = x < self.argmax // 2
        x = x.long()
        x = nn.functional.one_hot(x, num_classes=self.output_dim)
        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.mul_factor = mul_factor
        self.masked_fill_value = masked_fill_value

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, mod_input, div_input):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.mod_input = mod_input
        self.div_input = div_input

        result = torch.nonzero(result)
        result = torch.fmod(result, self.div_input)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_channels, divisor, nan_to_num_nan, nan_to_num_posinf, nan_to_num_neginf):
        super().__init__()
        self.divisor = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(divisor))
        self.nan_to_num_nan = nan_to_num_nan
        self.nan_to_num_posinf = nan_to_num_posinf
        self.nan_to_num_neginf = nan_to_num_neginf

        x = torch.div(x, self.divisor)
        x = torch.nan_to_num(x, nan=self.nan_to_num_nan, posinf=self.nan_to_num_posinf, neginf=self.nan_to_num_neginf)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        # Initialize a parameter that will be used in the forward pass
        self.param = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply sorting operation to the input tensor
        x = torch.argsort(x, dim=1, descending=True)
        # Apply absolute value operation to the input tensor
        x = torch.abs(x)
        # Add the parameter to the input tensor
        x = x + self.param
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, where_threshold, squeeze_dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.where_threshold = where_threshold
        self.squeeze_dim = squeeze_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # unsqueeze operation
        x = torch.unsqueeze(x, self.squeeze_dim)
        # where operation
        x = torch.where(x > self.where_threshold, x, torch.zeros_like(x))
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.batchnorm = nn.BatchNorm1d(num_features)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply batch normalization
        x = self.batchnorm(x)
        # Reshape x to allow interpolation
        x = x.view(x.size(0), x.size(1), -1)
        # Perform interpolation
        x = F.interpolate(x, scale_factor=2, mode="nearest")
        # Add bias
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, mul_factor: float, acos_eps: float):
        super().__init__()
        self.mul_factor = mul_factor
        self.acos_eps = acos_eps

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = torch.mul(x, self.mul_factor)
        x = torch.acos(torch.clamp(x, min=-1.0 + self.acos_eps, max=1.0 - self.acos_eps))

        self.max_value = max_value
        self.min_value = min_value

        x = torch.clip(x, self.min_value, self.max_value)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.channels = channels

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.min across the channels
        min_values = torch.min(x, dim=1)[0]
        # Apply torch.count_nonzero across the channels
        nonzero_counts = torch.count_nonzero(x, dim=1)
        # Concatenate the results along a new dimension
        output = torch.cat((min_values.unsqueeze(1), nonzero_counts.unsqueeze(1)), dim=1)
        return output

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, seq_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.seq_dim = seq_dim

    def forward(self, x, y):
        # x has shape (batch_size, seq_dim, feat_dim)
        # y has shape (batch_size, feat_dim, 1)
        out = torch.bmm(x, y)  # result has shape (batch_size, seq_dim, 1)
        out_amax = torch.amax(out, dim=1)  # find the max value for each sample in the batch
        return out_amax

        self.input_size = input_size
        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
        self.seq_length = seq_length

        super().__init__()
        self.num_classes = num_classes
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()

        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim_x, dim_y):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim_x = dim_x
        self.dim_y = dim_y

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        grid_x, grid_y = torch.meshgrid(torch.arange(self.dim_x), torch.arange(self.dim_y))
        indices = torch.stack([grid_x, grid_y], dim=-1)
        min_indices = torch.argmin(x, dim=-1)
        min_values = torch.gather(x, dim=-1, index=min_indices[..., None])
        return min_values

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, parameter_size):
        super().__init__()
        # Parameter is a simple nn.Module that stores a learnable parameter.
        # It can be used as a layer in a network if desired, but here we just use it to store a value.
        self.param = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(parameter_size))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Sigmoid is a non-linear function that scales and shifts the input
        # It is often used to ensure the output of a model is between 0 and 1
        x = torch.sigmoid(x)
        x = x + self.param
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, element_shape, test_elements_shape, fill_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.fill_value = fill_value
        self.test_elements = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(test_elements_shape))
        self.fill_tensor = nn.Parameter(torch.full(element_shape, fill_value))

    def forward(self, elements):
        mask = torch.isin(elements, self.test_elements)
        filled_elements = torch.where(mask, self.fill_tensor, elements)
        result = torch.minimum(filled_elements, self.test_elements)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, threshold):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.threshold = threshold

        result = torch.any(distance < self.threshold)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, negative_slope, power, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.relu = nn.ReLU()
        self.leaky_relu = nn.LeakyReLU(negative_slope)
        self.power = power
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.relu(x)
        x = self.leaky_relu(x)
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = torch.pow(x, self.power)  # equivalent to x ** self.power
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

        self.clip_min = clip_min

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_shape, threshold_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = nn.Parameter(torch.full(tensor_shape, threshold_value))

        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.upsample_scale_factor = upsample_scale_factor
        self.upsample_mode = upsample_mode

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels: int,
        out_channels: int,
        kernel_size: tuple,
        stride: int = 1,
        padding: tuple = (0, 0),
        dilation: tuple = (1, 1),
        bias: bool = False,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv2d = nn.Conv2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, dilation=dilation, bias=bias
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        eq_result = torch.eq(x, y)
        conv_result = self.conv2d(eq_result.float())
        return conv_result

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, out_dim, alpha):
        super().__init__()
        self.alpha = alpha

    def forward(self, x):
        # Create a tensor with a constant value across the whole input
        constant_tensor = torch.full_like(x, self.alpha)
        # Apply maximum with the constant tensor
        max_applied = torch.maximum(x, constant_tensor)
        # Apply power operation to the result
        result = torch.pow(max_applied, 2)
        return result

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.register_buffer("sigmoid_input", torch.tensor(0.0))
        self.stack_input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Sigmoid operation
        self.sigmoid_input.fill_(x.sum().item())
        sigmoid_output = torch.sigmoid(self.sigmoid_input)

        # Stack operation
        stack_output = torch.stack([sigmoid_output] * self.stack_input_size, dim=0)

        return stack_output

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold, reduction):
        super().__init__()
        self.kl_div_loss = nn.KLDivLoss(reduction=reduction)
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, input_preds, target_preds, input):

        where_mask = torch.where(loss > self.threshold, input, 0)
        return where_mask

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv_transpose(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        x = torch.minimum(x, torch.tensor(0.0))
        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min
        self.clamp_max = clamp_max

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, dilation, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv2d(
            in_channels,
            out_channels,
            kernel_size,
            stride=stride,
            padding=padding,
            dilation=dilation,
            bias=bool(bias_shape),
        )
        if bias_shape is not None:
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv(x)
        if hasattr(self, "bias"):
            x = torch.multiply(x, self.bias)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, mid_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.mid_dim = mid_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply acos to each channel independently
        x = torch.stack([torch.acos(xi) for xi in torch.unbind(x, dim=1)], dim=1)
        # Concatenate along the channel dimension
        x = torch.cat(torch.unbind(x, dim=1), dim=2)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold_value, subtract_value):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.threshold_value = torch.tensor(threshold_value)
        self.subtract_value = torch.tensor(subtract_value)

    def forward(self, x):
        condition = x < self.threshold_value
        x = torch.where(condition, x + self.subtract_value, x)
        x = torch.sub(x, self.subtract_value)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, divisor):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.divisor = divisor

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.triu to the input tensor
        x_tril = torch.tril(x)

        # Perform division by the divisor
        x = x_tril / self.divisor

        # Add a constant to the result
        x = x + 1.0

        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.bce_loss = nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss(reduction=reduction)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, k_value, num_channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.k_value = k_value
        self.num_channels = num_channels

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.topk (returns two outputs, but we only use the indices)
        topk_indices = torch.topk(x, self.k_value, dim=1)[1]

        # Apply torch.abs
        abs_topk_indices = torch.abs(topk_indices)

        return abs_topk_indices

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()

            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim

        y = y.view(self.batch_size, -1, self.embedding_dim)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size, batch_first=True):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
        self.batch_first = batch_first

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Check that the first dimension is not 'None', i.e., it is a known batch size
        if self.batch_first and x.size(0) is None or y.size(0) is None:
            raise ValueError("Batch dimension must be known")

        # Use torch.bmm to perform batch matrix multiplication
        result = torch.bmm(x, y)

        # Use torch.lt to perform element-wise less-than comparison
        mask = torch.lt(result, 0)

        # Apply mask to result tensor
        result = torch.masked_fill(result, mask, 0)

        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        # The order and parameters of these operations may vary based on the requirements
        # This is a placeholder for the code, the actual code should be written based on the specific use case

    def forward(self, x1: torch.Tensor, x2: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x1 = torch.roll(x1, shifts=(1, -1), dims=(2, 3))

        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, reduction):
        super().__init__()

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, divisor, exponential_base):
        super().__init__()
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.exponential_base = exponential_base

    def forward(self, x):
        # torch.div is used to divide the input tensor by a divisor
        x = torch.div(x, self.divisor)
        # torch.exp is used to compute the exponential of each element in the input tensor
        x = torch.exp(x * self.exponential_base)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, batch1, batch2, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.in_features = in_features
        self.batch1 = batch1
        self.batch2 = batch2
        self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        batch_matrix1 = torch.bmm(x, x.transpose(1, 2))
        batch_matrix2 = torch.bmm(x.transpose(1, 2), x)
        result = torch.baddbmm(self.bias, batch_matrix1, batch_matrix2)
        logical_or_result = torch.logical_or(result > 0, result < 1)
        return logical_or_result

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, input_dim, output_dim, repeat_interleave_dim, repeats):
        super().__init__()
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(batch_size, repeat_interleave_dim, output_dim))
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(batch_size, input_dim, output_dim))
        self.repeats = repeats

    def forward(self, batch1, batch2):
        batch2_repeated = torch.repeat_interleave(batch2, repeats=self.repeats, dim=1)
        result = torch.baddbmm(self.bias, batch1, batch2_repeated)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim1, dim2):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim1 = dim1
        self.dim2 = dim2

    def forward(self, a, b):
        grid_x, grid_y = torch.meshgrid(a, b, indexing="ij")
        result = torch.einsum("ij,ij->ij", grid_x, grid_y)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv_transpose(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=0.0, max=1.0)
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, mask_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.mask_value = mask_value

    def forward(self, x):
        mean_value = torch.mean(x)
        x = torch.masked_fill(x, x == self.mask_value, mean_value)
        return x

        super().__init__()

        super().__init__()
        self.vector_length = vector_length
        self.eps = eps

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = x.view(-1, self.vector_length)

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()

        self.batch_size = batch_size

        self.k = k

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_min = min_val
        self.clamp_max = max_val
        self.pad_l = pad_l
        self.pad_r = pad_r
        self.pad_t = pad_t

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

        x = F.conv1d(

            self.conv.weight,
            self.conv.bias,
            self.conv.stride,
            self.conv.padding,
            self.conv.dilation,

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_channels, topk_k):
        super().__init__()
        self.topk_k = topk_k

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply topk to each channel independently
        _, indices = torch.topk(x, k=self.topk_k, dim=1)
        # Use the indices to index the maximum values in each channel
        max_val_per_channel = torch.gather(x, 1, indices)
        # Apply hardswish activation
        return nn.Hardswish()(max_val_per_channel)

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        num_features: int,
        eps: float = 1e-05,
        momentum: float = 0.1,
        affine: bool = True,
        track_running_stats: bool = True,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_norm = nn.BatchNorm1d(
            num_features, eps=eps, momentum=momentum, affine=affine, track_running_stats=track_running_stats
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.batch_norm(x)
        x = torch.stack([x, x])  # Stack along the 0th dimension (batch dimension)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dividend_channels, divisor_channels, divisor):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.dividend_channels = dividend_channels
        self.divisor_channels = divisor_channels
        self.divisor = divisor

    def forward(self, x):
        # Expect x to be a tensor of shape [batch_size, self.dividend_channels, height, width]
        # Apply torch.fmod for each channel independently with a constant divisor
        fmod_results = torch.fmod(x, self.divisor)

        # Apply torch.where to each tensor in the batch separately
        # The condition is true if the result is zero, otherwise false
        # The result for the condition true is dividend, and for the condition false is divisor
        where_results = torch.where(fmod_results == 0.0, x, self.divisor)

        return where_results

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, threshold_op_value, erf_scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.in_features = in_features
        self.out_features = out_features
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.threshold_op_value = threshold_op_value
        self.erf_scaling_factor = erf_scaling_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        threshold_condition = x < self.threshold_op_value
        x = torch.where(threshold_condition, x, torch.zeros_like(x))
        x = x * self.erf_scaling_factor
        x = torch.erf(x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features: int, out_features: int, padding_width: int, padding_value: float):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.padding_width = padding_width
        self.padding_value = padding_value

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = torch.matmul(x, self.linear.weight.t()) + self.linear.bias
        x = F.pad(x, (self.padding_width, self.padding_width), mode="constant", value=self.padding_value)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, split_size, boundaries):
        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.boundaries = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(boundaries, dtype=torch.float32), requires_grad=False)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=1)
        x = torch.cat([torch.bucketize(sub_x, self.boundaries).float() for sub_x in x], dim=1)

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        # Concatenate the two tensors along the specified dimension
        combined_tensor = torch.cat((x1, x2), dim=self.dim)
        # Find the argmax over the combined tensor
        argmax_result = torch.argmax(combined_tensor, dim=self.dim)
        return argmax_result

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, max_value, min_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_value = max_value
        self.min_value = min_value

    def forward(self, input_tensor, mask_tensor):

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, repeat_times, cond, repeat_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.repeat_times = repeat_times
        self.cond = cond
        self.repeat_shape = repeat_shape

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, self.repeat_times, dim=0)
        cond_mask = torch.ge(x, self.cond)
        x = x * cond_mask
        x = x.repeat(self.repeat_shape)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, start, end, step, num_samples):
        super().__init__()
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.step = step
        self.num_samples = num_samples

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use torch.arange to create a sequence of numbers
        sequence = torch.arange(self.start, self.end, self.step, device=x.device)
        # Use torch.sinc to apply the sinc function element-wise to the input tensor
        x = x * torch.sinc(sequence[: self.num_samples])
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x, y):
        mask = torch.logical_and(x > self.threshold, y > self.threshold)
        x = F.silu(x)
        y = F.silu(y)
        return x * mask.to(x.dtype), y * mask.to(y.dtype)

            self.conv1d.bias,
            self.conv1d.stride,
            self.conv1d.padding,
            self.conv1d.dilation,

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings, embedding_dim, kernel_size, padding):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim)
        self.conv1d = nn.Conv1d(embedding_dim, embedding_dim, kernel_size, padding=padding)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.embedding(x)  # (B, T) -> (B, T, embedding_dim)
        x = x.transpose(1, 2)  # (B, T, embedding_dim) -> (B, embedding_dim, T)
        x = F.conv1d(x, self.conv1d.weight, bias=self.conv1d.bias, padding=self.conv1d.padding)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, power_value):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.power_value = power_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.sort to the input tensor along the last dimension
        sorted_x, _ = torch.sort(x, dim=-1)
        # Apply torch.pow to each element of the sorted tensor to a specified power
        powered_x = torch.pow(sorted_x, self.power_value)
        return powered_x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, dim, repeats):
        super().__init__()
        self.kldivloss = nn.KLDivLoss(reduction="none")
        self.dim = dim
        self.repeats = repeats

    def forward(self, input, target):
        input = F.log_softmax(input, dim=self.dim)
        target = F.softmax(target, dim=self.dim)
        loss = self.kldivloss(input, target)
        loss = torch.repeat_interleave(loss, self.repeats, dim=self.dim)
        return loss

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, other_dims):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.other_dims = other_dims

    def forward(self, A: torch.Tensor, B: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # A should be of shape (batch_size, other_dims, other_dims)
        # B should be of shape (batch_size, other_dims, other_dims)
        assert A.shape == (self.batch_size, self.other_dims, self.other_dims)
        assert B.shape == (self.batch_size, self.other_dims, self.other_dims)

        # Use torch.bmm to perform batch matrix multiplication
        C = torch.bmm(A, B)

        # Apply sigmoid to get output
        C = nn.Sigmoid()(C)
        return C

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.scaling_factor = scaling_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.tril to the input tensor
        tril_x = torch.tril(x)
        # Apply torch.maximum to the input tensor and its triangular counterpart
        max_x = torch.maximum(x, tril_x)
        # Scale the result
        scaled_max_x = max_x * self.scaling_factor
        return scaled_max_x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, seq_length, num_heads):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.seq_length = seq_length
        self.num_heads = num_heads

    def forward(
        self, query: torch.Tensor, key: torch.Tensor, value: torch.Tensor, mask: torch.Tensor = None
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        attention_output = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(query, key, value, attn_mask=mask)
        logical_not_output = torch.logical_not(attention_output)
        return logical_not_output

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features):
        super().__init__()
        self.module_dict = nn.ModuleDict(
            {"linear1": nn.Linear(in_features, out_features), "linear2": nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)}
        )

    def forward(self, x, indices, src):
        x1 = self.module_dict["linear1"](x)
        x2 = self.module_dict["linear2"](x)
        x = x1 + x2
        x = torch.scatter(x, 1, indices, src)

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.tensor1 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(shape_tensor1))
        self.tensor2 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(shape_tensor2))

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, embedding_dim, p):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
        self.p = p

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply torch.count_nonzero
        nonzero_count = torch.count_nonzero(x, dim=1)

        # Apply torch.norm
        norm = torch.norm(x, p=self.p, dim=1)

        return norm, nonzero_count

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, divisor, logarithm_base):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.logarithm_base = logarithm_base

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.remainder operation
        x = torch.remainder(x, self.divisor)
        # Apply torch.log10 operation
        x = torch.log10(x) / math.log10(self.logarithm_base)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, bias_bool):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features, bias=bias_bool)

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        grid = torch.meshgrid([x1, x2], indexing="ij")
        combined_grid = torch.cat([grid[0].flatten().unsqueeze(1), grid[1].flatten().unsqueeze(1)], dim=1)
        output = F.linear(combined_grid, self.linear.weight, self.linear.bias)
        return output

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.step = step
        self.act = nn.SiLU()

    def forward(self, x):

        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min
        self.clamp_max = clamp_max
        self.neg_value = neg_value

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.clamp_min, max=self.clamp_max)
        x = torch.neg(x)

        return x

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use topk to get the k largest values and their indices.
        topk_values, topk_indices = torch.topk(x, k=self.k_topk)

        # Create a mask where values are above the threshold.
        mask = torch.where(x > self.threshold, torch.tensor(1.0), torch.tensor(0.0))

        # Apply the mask to the input tensor.
        masked_x = x * mask

        # Replace the k largest values in the masked tensor with the topk values.
        masked_x = torch.where(masked_x == 0, topk_values, masked_x)

        return masked_x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, normalized_shape, device, dtype):
        super().__init__()
        self.layernorm = nn.LayerNorm(normalized_shape, device=device, dtype=dtype)

    def forward(self, x, height, width):
        grid_x, grid_y = torch.meshgrid(torch.arange(height), torch.arange(width), indexing="ij")
        x = x + grid_x.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0).to(x.device)
        x = x + grid_y.unsqueeze(0).unsqueeze(0).to(x.device)
        x = self.layernorm(x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, split_size, min_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.min_value = min_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Split the input tensor
        x_split = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=self.input_size)

        # Apply clamp_min to each split tensor
        x_clamped = [torch.clamp_min(t, min=self.min_value) for t in x_split]

        # Concatenate the clamped tensors along the specified dimension
        x_out = torch.cat(x_clamped, dim=self.input_size)

        return x_out

    def __init__(self, input_size, power, mask_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.power = power
        self.mask_value = mask_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.pow (raise each element to the specified power)
        x = torch.pow(x, self.power)
        # Create a mask for x. If x < 0, replace with mask_value

        x = torch.masked_fill(x, mask, self.mask_value)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, catdim, rsqrt_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim = dim
        self.catdim = catdim
        self.rsqrt_dim = rsqrt_dim

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        x = torch.cat((x1, x2), dim=self.catdim)
        x = torch.rsqrt(torch.abs(x) + 1e-5)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, kernel_size: int, stride: int, padding: int, dilation: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.kernel_size = kernel_size
        self.stride = stride
        self.padding = padding
        self.dilation = dilation

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = torch.ceil(x)
        x = F.unfold(x, kernel_size=self.kernel_size, stride=self.stride, padding=self.padding, dilation=self.dilation)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes, maxlen):
        super().__init__()
        self.maxlen = maxlen
        self.num_classes = num_classes

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x = F.one_hot(x, num_classes=self.num_classes)
        y = F.one_hot(y, num_classes=self.num_classes)
        x = torch.logical_or(x, y)
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings, embedding_dim, roll_shifts, roll_dims):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim)
        self.roll_shifts = roll_shifts
        self.roll_dims = roll_dims

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.embedding(x, self.embedding.weight)
        x = torch.roll(x, shifts=self.roll_shifts, dims=self.roll_dims)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.pixel_shuffle = nn.PixelShuffle(upscale_factor)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, shift_amount):
        super().__init__()
        self.roll_amount = shift_amount
        self.batchnorm = nn.BatchNorm3d(num_features)

    def forward(self, x):

        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        emb_dim,
        emb_num_embeddings,
        emb_padding_idx,
        emb_max_norm,
        emb_norm_type,
        emb_scale_grad_by_freq,
        emb_sparse,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(
            emb_num_embeddings,
            emb_dim,
            padding_idx=emb_padding_idx,
            max_norm=emb_max_norm,
            norm_type=emb_norm_type,
            scale_grad_by_freq=emb_scale_grad_by_freq,
            sparse=emb_sparse,
        )
        self.tanh = nn.Tanh()

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.embedding(x)
        x = self.tanh(x)
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_a_shape, tensor_b_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.tensor_a = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(tensor_a_shape))
        self.tensor_b = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(tensor_b_shape))

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, normalized_shape, eps, start, end):
        super().__init__()
        self.layernorm = nn.LayerNorm(normalized_shape, eps=eps)
        self.start = start
        self.end = end

    def forward(self, x):
        shape = x.shape
        x = self.layernorm(x)
        arange_tensor = torch.arange(self.start, self.end).view(1, -1).repeat(shape[0], 1).to(x.device)
        return x, arange_tensor

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv3d = nn.Conv3d(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv3d(x)
        x = x + self.bias  # bias added after conv3d to maintain 3D parameters
        x = torch.clamp_min(x, min=0.0)  # Clamping after addition to maintain non-negative values
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, start, end, steps, batch_size, m, n, k):
        super().__init__()
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.steps = steps
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.m = m
        self.n = n
        self.k = k

    def forward(self, A, B, C):
        alpha = torch.linspace(self.start, self.end, self.steps)[0]
        beta = torch.linspace(self.start, self.end, self.steps)[1]
        return alpha * torch.baddbmm(C, A, B, beta=beta, alpha=alpha)
