import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, repeats, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.repeats = repeats
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, self.repeats, dim=self.dim)
        x = torch.softmax(x, dim=self.dim)
        return x

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.fc = nn.Linear(in_features, in_features)

    def forward(self, x):

        self.pool = nn.AvgPool1d(kernel_size=in_channels)

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, start, end, steps, divisor, out_channels, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.steps = steps
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.out_channels = out_channels
        self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.linspace(self.start, self.end, steps=self.steps, dtype=x.dtype, device=x.device)

        x = x + self.bias
        return x

        self.condition = condition
        self.x = x

        x = x * self.scalar_value

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, divisor, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.log_softmax = F.log_softmax
        self.fmod = torch.fmod
        self.in_features = in_features
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply LogSoftmax
        x = self.log_softmax(x, dim=self.in_features)
        # Apply Fmod
        x = self.fmod(x, self.divisor)
        # Apply Thresholding
        x = torch.where(x > self.threshold, x, torch.tensor(self.threshold))
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, num_splits):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.num_splits = num_splits

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.condition = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(condition_shape))
        self.input = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(input_shape))

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_groups, num_channels, threshold, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.gn = nn.GroupNorm(num_groups, num_channels)
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

        x = torch.ge(x, self.threshold)
        x = x.to(torch.float32)
        x = self.gn(x)
        return x

        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, k_value, add_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.k_value = k_value
        self.add_value = add_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply topk operation
        topk_values, topk_indices = torch.topk(x, k=self.k_value)
        # Add a value to the topk values
        result = torch.add(topk_values, self.add_value)
        return result

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, acos_dim, cumsum_dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.acos_dim = acos_dim
        self.cumsum_dim = cumsum_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.acos
        x_acos = torch.acos(torch.clamp(x, min=-1.0, max=1.0))

        # Apply torch.cumsum
        x_cumsum = torch.cumsum(x_acos, dim=self.cumsum_dim)

        return x_cumsum

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, emb_size, output_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed = nn.Linear(emb_size, output_dim)

    def forward(self, x, y):

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.cumsum(x, self.dim)
        grid = torch.meshgrid(*[torch.arange(size) for size in x.shape[1:]], indexing="ij")
        grid = torch.stack(grid, dim=0)
        x = x + grid.to(x.device)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, weight_shape, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(weight_shape))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x1, x2, value):
        eq_result = torch.eq(x1, x2)
        addcmul_result = torch.addcmul(eq_result.to(dtype=torch.float32), self.weight, self.bias, value=value)
        return addcmul_result

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, k: int, min_val: float, max_val: float):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.k = k
        self.min_val = min_val
        self.max_val = max_val

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor):
        # Apply topk operation
        top_values, top_indices = torch.topk(x, k=self.k)

        # Apply clip operation
        clipped_values = torch.clip(top_values, min=self.min_val, max=self.max_val)

        return clipped_values, top_indices

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, upscale_factor, shape_change_channels, channels_shrinking_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.pixelshuffle = nn.PixelShuffle(upscale_factor)
        self.masked_fill = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(0.0))
        self.shape_change_channels = shape_change_channels
        self.channels_shrinking_factor = channels_shrinking_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.pixelshuffle(x)
        mask = torch.zeros_like(x)
        mask[:, : self.shape_change_channels, :, :] = 1
        x = torch.masked_fill(x, mask == 0, self.masked_fill)
        x = x / self.channels_shrinking_factor
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, output_dim, mask_value):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.mask_value = mask_value

    def forward(self, x):
        max_value, _ = torch.max(x, dim=1, keepdim=True)
        mask = x < max_value * self.mask_value
        x = torch.masked_fill(x, mask, 0.0)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, bias_shape, log_scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))
        self.log_scaling_factor = log_scaling_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clamp_min(x, min=0.0)
        x = torch.log(x + 1e-6)  # Adding a small epsilon to avoid log(0)
        x = x + self.bias
        x = torch.clamp_min(x, min=0.0)
        x = x * self.log_scaling_factor
        x = torch.clamp_min(x, min=0.0)
        x = x / self.log_scaling_factor
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.loss_fn = nn.KLDivLoss(reduction="none")  # no reduction for traceability

    def forward(self, pred: torch.Tensor, target: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        pred = torch.rsqrt(pred)
        loss = self.loss_fn(pred, target)
        return loss

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_features):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_features = input_features

        return result

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias_shape, scaling_factor
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.param = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))
        self.adaptive_avg_pool2d = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(output_size=(1, 1))
        self.scaling_factor = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(scaling_factor))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.adaptive_avg_pool2d(x, output_size=(1, 1))
        x = x + self.param
        x = x * self.scaling_factor
        return x

        self.layer_norm = nn.LayerNorm(normalized_shape, eps, elementwise_affine)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super().__init__()
        # No nn layers are used, so we don't need in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, etc.
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.expm1 operation to the input tensor
        x = torch.expm1(x)
        # Apply torch.argmin operation to the input tensor
        x = torch.argmin(x, dim=self.input_size - 1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, norm_dim, any_dim, norm_p):
        super().__init__()
        self.norm_p = norm_p
        self.input_dim = input_dim
        self.any_dim = any_dim
        self.norm_dim = norm_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        x = x.view(self.input_dim, self.norm_dim)
        norm = torch.norm(x, p=self.norm_p)
        any_tensor = torch.any(x != 0, dim=self.any_dim)
        x = norm * any_tensor
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, weight_shape, bias_shape):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.weight = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*weight_shape))
        self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.addmm(self.bias, x, self.weight)
        x = torch.count_nonzero(x)
        return x

        self.clamp_max = clamp_max

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, device, tensor_a, tensor_b):
        super().__init__()
        self.tensor_a = nn.Parameter(tensor_a.to(device), requires_grad=False)
        self.tensor_b = nn.Parameter(tensor_b.to(device), requires_grad=False)

    def forward(self, x):
        dist = torch.dist(self.tensor_a, self.tensor_b)
        x = torch.erf(x * dist)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.softplus = nn.Softplus()
        self.params = nn.ParameterDict(
            {
                "param1": nn.Parameter(torch.randn(in_features)),
                "param2": nn.Parameter(torch.randn(in_features)),
                # You can add more parameters here if needed
            }
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply softplus to x
        x = self.softplus(x)
        # Add parameters from the parameter dictionary
        x += self.params["param1"]
        x += self.params["param2"]
        return x

        self.mul = lambda x, y: x * y
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding

        x = self.mul(x, 2)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, bias_shape, shifts):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, bias=True)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))
        self.shifts = shifts

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.roll(x, shifts=self.shifts, dims=[2])
        x = F.conv1d(
            x, self.conv.weight, self.conv.bias + self.bias, stride=self.conv.stride, padding=self.conv.padding
        )
        return x

        self.threshold_value1 = threshold_value1
        self.threshold_value2 = threshold_value2

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use torch.ge to get elements greater than or equal to the threshold
        ge_result = torch.ge(x, self.threshold)
        # Use torch.histc to compute the histogram of the data
        histc_result = torch.histc(ge_result.float(), bins=2, min=0, max=1)
        return histc_result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings1, num_embeddings2, embedding_dim, padding_idx1, padding_idx2):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding1 = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings1, embedding_dim, padding_idx=padding_idx1)
        self.embedding2 = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings2, embedding_dim, padding_idx=padding_idx2)

    def forward(self, indices1, indices2):
        x1 = F.embedding(indices1, self.embedding1.weight, padding_idx=self.embedding1.padding_idx)
        x2 = F.embedding(indices2, self.embedding2.weight, padding_idx=self.embedding2.padding_idx)
        x = torch.cat((x1, x2), dim=1)
        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.num_chunks = num_chunks

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Compute argmax along the specified dimension

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x, target):
        loss = F.l1_loss(x, target, reduction="none")
        mask = torch.where(loss > self.threshold, torch.tensor(1.0), torch.tensor(0.0))
        x = x * mask
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):

        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.limit = limit
        self.value = value
        self.alpha = alpha

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, condition_shape, einsum_dims):
        super().__init__()
        self.condition = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*condition_shape))
        self.einsum_dims = einsum_dims

    def forward(self, x):
        condition_broadcasted = self.condition.unsqueeze(-1).unsqueeze(-1)
        x = torch.where(condition_broadcasted < 0, x, 0)
        x = torch.einsum(self.einsum_dims, x)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, batch_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.weight = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(batch_size, input_size, input_size))
        torch.nn.init.uniform_(self.weight, -0.1, 0.1)

    def forward(self, x):
        # Batch matrix multiplication
        bmm_result = torch.bmm(x, self.weight)
        # Element-wise division
        result = torch.div(bmm_result, x)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, condition_shape, in1_shape, in2_shape, in3_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.condition = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(condition_shape))
        self.in1 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(in1_shape))
        self.in2 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(in2_shape))
        self.in3 = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(in3_shape))

    def forward(self, x):

        x = torch.addcmul(x, self.in3, self.in1, value=2)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, clip_value, loss_weight):
        super().__init__()
        self.l1_loss = nn.L1Loss(reduction="mean")
        self.clip_value = clip_value
        self.loss_weight = loss_weight

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x = torch.clip(x, min=-self.clip_value, max=self.clip_value)
        loss = self.loss_weight * self.l1_loss(x, y)
        return loss

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features: int, out_features: int, use_bias: bool = True):
        super().__init__()
        self.mul = lambda x, y: x * y
        self.tanh = torch.tanh
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features, bias=use_bias)
        self.in_features = in_features
        self.out_features = out_features

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = x.view(-1, self.in_features)  # Flatten the tensor
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = self.mul(x, self.out_features)  # Multiply by out_features
        x = self.tanh(x)
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        # Use torch.gt to create a mask where x > threshold
        mask = torch.gt(x, self.threshold)
        # Apply SiLU activation function to x where mask is True
        x = torch.where(mask, F.silu(x), x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, clip_value, temperature):
        super().__init__()
        self.clip_value = clip_value
        self.temperature = temperature

        x = F.softmax(x / self.temperature, dim=1)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.slope = nn.Parameter(torch.rand(1))

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_rows: int, num_cols: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_rows = num_rows
        self.num_cols = num_cols

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Ensure the input tensor has the correct shape
        assert x.ndim == 2 and x.shape[1] == self.num_cols, "Input tensor must be of shape [num_rows, num_cols]"

        # Element-wise sum
        x = torch.sum(x, dim=1, keepdim=True)

        # Cumulative sum over each row
        x = torch.cumsum(x, dim=0)
        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.division_factor = division_factor
        self.exponent = exponent

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim1: int, dim2: int):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.dim1 = dim1
        self.dim2 = dim2

    def forward(self, input1: torch.Tensor, input2: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Generate grid of indices for the first input tensor
        grid1, grid2 = torch.meshgrid(
            torch.arange(input1.size(self.dim1)), torch.arange(input1.size(self.dim2)), indexing="ij"
        )
        grid1 = grid1.to(input1.device).to(input1.dtype)
        grid2 = grid2.to(input1.device).to(input1.dtype)

        # Use the maximum operation on the input tensors along the specified dimensions
        max_values = torch.maximum(input1 + grid1, input2 + grid2)
        return max_values

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, reduction="mean"):
        super().__init__()
        self.bcewithlogitsloss = nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss(reduction=reduction)

    def forward(self, logits, targets):
        bce_loss = self.bcewithlogitsloss(logits, targets)
        l1_loss = F.smooth_l1_loss(logits, targets)

        return total_loss

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, k_top, max_value, min_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.k_top = k_top
        self.max_value = max_value
        self.min_value = min_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Top-k
        x, _ = torch.topk(x, self.k_top, dim=1)
        # Clip

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold, multiplier):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.multiplier = multiplier

    def forward(self, x):
        # torch.ge(input, other, out=None) → Tensor
        # Computes element-wise inequality comparisons, where the comparison is 'greater than or equal to'.
        greater_than_equal = torch.ge(x, self.threshold)

        # torch.mul(input, other, out=None) → Tensor
        # Multiplies each element of the input by each element of the other, and returns a tensor with the result.
        multiplied = torch.mul(greater_than_equal, self.multiplier)
        return multiplied

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv_transpose(x)
        mask = (x > 0).float()
        x = torch.masked_fill(x, mask == 0, 0)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, weight_shape, bias_shape, value):
        super().__init__()
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(weight_shape))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))
        self.value = value

    def forward(self, x, y):
        similarity = F.cosine_similarity(x, y, dim=1)
        result = torch.addcmul(similarity, self.weight, self.bias, value=self.value)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.module_dict = nn.ModuleDict(module_dict)
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min

        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.clamp_min, max=self.clamp_max)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_bins, min_tensor_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.bins = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(num_bins).sort().values)
        self.min_tensor = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(min_tensor_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x_min = torch.min(x, 1, keepdim=True).values
        x_min_clamped = x_min + self.min_tensor
        x_bucketized = torch.bucketize(x_min_clamped, self.bins)
        return x_bucketized

        super().__init__()
        self.clamp = nn.Softplus()
        self.kl_div_loss = nn.KLDivLoss(reduction=reduction)
        self.min_val = min_val
        self.max_val = max_val

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, acos_factor, fmod_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.acos_factor = acos_factor
        self.fmod_factor = fmod_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        # Ensure the input tensor is of the correct size
        if x.shape != self.input_size:
            raise ValueError("Input tensor shape does not match the model's expected shape.")

        # Apply torch.acos element-wise followed by division by a factor
        acos_applied = torch.acos(x) / self.acos_factor

        # Apply torch.fmod element-wise followed by multiplication by a factor
        result = torch.fmod(acos_applied, self.fmod_factor)

        return result

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor1_shape, tensor2_shape, tensor3_shape, scalar):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.tensor1 = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*tensor1_shape))
        self.tensor2 = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*tensor2_shape))
        self.tensor3 = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*tensor3_shape))
        self.scalar = scalar

    def forward(self, input_tensor):
        output = torch.remainder(input_tensor, self.tensor1)
        output = torch.addcmul(output, self.tensor2, self.tensor3, value=self.scalar)
        return output

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, repeat_dim, repeat_interleave_count, index_select_dim, index_select_indices):
        super().__init__()
        self.repeat_dim = repeat_dim
        self.repeat_interleave_count = repeat_interleave_count
        self.index_select_dim = index_select_dim
        self.index_select_indices = index_select_indices
        self.register_buffer("indices", torch.tensor(index_select_indices))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, self.repeat_interleave_count, dim=self.repeat_dim)
        x = torch.index_select(x, self.index_select_dim, self.indices)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, subs_shape, eq_shape, threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.subs_shape = subs_shape
        self.eq_shape = eq_shape

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x = torch.sub(x, y)  # subtract x and y
        x = torch.eq(x, self.threshold)  # element-wise equality comparison with threshold
        x = x.view(self.eq_shape)  # reshape the tensor
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, stacked_dims):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_dim = input_dim
        self.stacked_dims = stacked_dims

    def forward(self, x):
        # Split the input tensor along the first dimension
        x_split = torch.split(x, self.input_dim, dim=0)
        # Stack the split tensors along the second dimension
        x_stacked = torch.stack(x_split, dim=self.stacked_dims)
        # Apply SiLU activation function element-wise
        x_silu = F.silu(x_stacked)
        return x_silu

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        # Initializers
        self.init_weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, requires_grad=True))
        self.init_bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, requires_grad=True))
        self.div_factor = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, requires_grad=True))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Forward pass
        x = F.kl_div(x, torch.full_like(x, 0.5), reduction="none")  # F.kl_div
        x = torch.where(x < self.init_weight, x, self.init_weight)  # torch.where
        x = x - self.init_bias  # sub
        x = x / self.div_factor  # div
        return x

    # Inputs
    return [torch.randn(10, 10, requires_grad=True)]

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, einsum_input, einsum_op, isnan_input):
        super().__init__()
        self.einsum_input = einsum_input
        self.einsum_op = einsum_op
        self.isnan_input = isnan_input

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.einsum(self.einsum_input, x)
        x = torch.isnan(x)
        x = torch.einsum(self.einsum_op, x)
        x = torch.isnan(x)
        x = torch.einsum(self.isnan_input, x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, attention_head_size, scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.attention_head_size = attention_head_size
        self.scaling_factor = scaling_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.unsqueeze
        x = torch.unsqueeze(x, dim=1)

        # Prepare the input for scaled_dot_product_attention
        query = key = value = x
        mask = None

        # Apply F.scaled_dot_product_attention
        x = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(query, key, value, attn_mask=mask, dropout_p=0.0, scale=self.scaling_factor)

        # Remove the extra dimension that was added by torch.unsqueeze
        x = torch.squeeze(x, dim=1)

        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        weight = x.view(self.batch_size, self.input_size, -1)
        other = x.view(self.batch_size, -1, self.input_size)

        return output

    def __init__(self, num_features, eps=1e-5, momentum=0.1, affine=False, track_running_stats=True):
        super().__init__()
        self.hardtanh = nn.Hardtanh()
        self.instancenorm3d = nn.InstanceNorm3d(num_features, eps, momentum, affine, track_running_stats)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.hardtanh(x)
        x = self.instancenorm3d(x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, index_dim, output_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.index_dim = index_dim
        self.output_dim = output_dim

    def forward(self, x, index):

        x = nn.Tanh()(x)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, bias_shape):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.expm1_param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(input_size))
        self.argsort_param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(input_size))
        self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.expm1(self.expm1_param)
        x = torch.argsort(self.argsort_param)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv_transpose(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        x = torch.cumsum(x, dim=1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, input_shape, weight_shape, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim = dim
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(weight_shape))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, input):
        x = F.softmax(input, dim=self.dim)
        x = torch.addcmul(x, self.weight, self.bias)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels: int, hidden_channels: int, out_channels: int, kernel_size: int, bias_shape: tuple):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv2d = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size)
        self.gelu = nn.GELU()
        self.conv2d_out = nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, out_channels, kernel_size)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.conv2d(x)
        x = self.gelu(x)
        x = self.conv2d_out(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_channels, num_prelu_features, alpha_init: float = 0.25):
        super().__init__()
        self.logical_and = torch.logical_and
        self.prelu = nn.PReLU(num_prelu_features, init=alpha_init)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.prelu(x)
        x = self.logical_and(x, x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, hidden_features, out_features):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear_layer = nn.Linear(in_features, hidden_features)
        self.out_layer = nn.Linear(hidden_features, out_features)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear_layer(x)
        x = F.gelu(x)  # GELU activation
        x = F.hardsigmoid(x)
        x = F.mish(x)  # Mish activation
        x = self.out_layer(x)
        return x

import torch

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, pad, std_dev):
        super().__init__()
        self.pad = nn.ConstantPad1d(pad, 0)
        self.std_dev = std_dev

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.pad(x)
        x = torch.std(x, dim=1, unbiased=False)
        x = x + self.std_dev
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, seq_length, diagonal_offset):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.seq_length = seq_length
        self.diagonal_offset = diagonal_offset

    def forward(self, x, y):
        y_tril = torch.tril(y, diagonal=self.diagonal_offset)
        output = torch.minimum(x, y_tril)
        return output

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(input_size, hidden_size)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(hidden_size, input_size)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.fc1(x)  # This applies a linear transformation to the input tensor
        x = torch.abs(x)  # This applies absolute value element-wise to the result
        x = self.fc2(x)  # This applies another linear transformation
        x = torch.mm(x, x.t())  # This performs matrix multiplication
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels,
        pool_kernel_size,
        pool_stride,
        pool_padding,
        pool_dilation,
        pool_ceil_mode,
        pool_return_indices,
        mish_channels,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_pool_3d = nn.MaxPool3d(
            kernel_size=pool_kernel_size,
            stride=pool_stride,
            padding=pool_padding,
            dilation=pool_dilation,
            ceil_mode=pool_ceil_mode,
            return_indices=pool_return_indices,
        )
        self.mish = nn.Mish()

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.max_pool_3d(x)
        x = self.mish(x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, shift_amount):
        super().__init__()
        self.shift_amount = shift_amount
        self.flatten = nn.Flatten()

        x = self.flatten(x)
        return x

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels, kernel_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.kernel_size = kernel_size
        self.instance_norm = nn.InstanceNorm2d(channels, affine=True)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(x)  # ReLU operation
        x = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=self.kernel_size)  # Max pooling operation
        x = self.instance_norm(x)  # Instance normalization operation
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, divisor, std_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.std_size = std_size

    def forward(self, x):
        # Reshape x to the predefined input size
        x = x.view(self.input_size)

        # Calculate the remainder of each element in x divided by the divisor
        x = torch.remainder(x, self.divisor)

        # Calculate the standard deviation of x along the given size
        x = torch.std(x, self.std_size)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, boundaries, output_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.layernorm = nn.LayerNorm(num_features)
        self.boundaries = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(boundaries).float(), requires_grad=False)
        self.output_size = output_size

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.layernorm(x)
        x = torch.bucketize(x, self.boundaries, right=False)
        x = F.one_hot(x, num_classes=self.output_size).float()
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim=0, descending=True):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.dim = dim
        self.descending = descending

    def forward(self, x):
        # argsort operation
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(x, dim=self.dim, descending=self.descending)
        sorted_tensor = torch.gather(x, self.dim, sorted_indices)

        # exp operation
        exp_tensor = torch.exp(sorted_tensor)
        return exp_tensor

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, scale_factor, mode):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_pool = nn.MaxPool2d(kernel_size=2, stride=2)
        self.upsample = nn.Upsample(scale_factor=scale_factor, mode=mode)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.max_pool(x)
        x_max, _ = torch.max(x, dim=1, keepdim=True)
        x = x_max.expand(-1, out_channels, -1, -1)
        x = self.upsample(x)
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim_a, dim_b, exponent):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.a = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(dim_a))
        self.b = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(dim_b))

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, topk_k):
        super().__init__()
        self.topk_k = topk_k

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply top k operation
        _, indices = torch.topk(x, k=self.topk_k, dim=1)
        x = torch.gather(x, dim=1, index=indices)

        x = F.silu(x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, param_dict_size):
        super().__init__()
        # Initialize the ParameterDict with some parameters
        self.param_dict = nn.ParameterDict(param_dict_size)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply logical_not to the input tensor element-wise
        x = torch.logical_not(x)
        # Use the ParameterDict to manage parameters
        for param_name, param in self.param_dict.items():
            x = x + param  # Assuming a simple operation for demonstration
        return x

        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.num_splits = num_splits

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, meshgrid_dims):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.meshgrid_dims = meshgrid_dims

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        grid = torch.meshgrid(*[torch.linspace(-1, 1, steps=s) for s in self.meshgrid_dims])
        x = torch.stack(grid, dim=-1).to(x.device)
        x = x + x
        x = x * x
        return x

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dividend_shape, divisor, mod_shape, mul_shape, add_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.dividend = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(dividend_shape))
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.mod = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(mod_shape))
        self.mul_factor = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(mul_shape))
        self.add = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(add_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.fmod(self.dividend, self.divisor)  # modulo operation
        x = torch.addcmul(self.mod, x, self.mul_factor)  # add and multiply operation
        x = x + self.add
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, shift, dim, p, dim_out):
        super().__init__()
        self.shift = shift
        self.dim = dim

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.comparison_threshold = comparison_threshold

        super().__init__()
        self.clip_value = clip_value

        super().__init__()
        self.softplus = F.softplus

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, x_shape, clip_min, clip_max, y_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.x_shape = x_shape
        self.clip_min = clip_min
        self.clip_max = clip_max
        self.y = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(y_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clip(x, min=self.clip_min, max=self.clip_max)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size, output_size, pool_kernel_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
        self.output_size = output_size
        self.pool_kernel_size = pool_kernel_size
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(hidden_size, input_size))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(hidden_size))
        self.adaptive_avg_pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool1d(output_size)

    def forward(self, x, mat):
        x = torch.addmm(self.bias, x, self.weight.t())
        x = self.adaptive_avg_pool(x.unsqueeze(0)).squeeze(0)
        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

import torch

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, clamp_val, softmax_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_val = clamp_val
        self.softmax_dim = softmax_dim

    def forward(self, x):

        x = torch.softmax(x, dim=self.softmax_dim)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, x_shape, y_shape, kernel_size, dilation, padding, stride):
        super().__init__()
        self.x = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(x_shape))
        self.y = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(y_shape))

    def forward(self, threshold):
        x_ge = torch.ge(self.x, threshold)
        x_unfold = F.unfold(x_ge.float(), kernel_size=kernel_size, dilation=dilation, padding=padding, stride=stride)
        return x_unfold

class Model(nn.Module):

        x = self.leaky_relu(x)
        x = torch.sub(x, 0.5)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold, scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold  # Threshold value for torch.gt operation
        self.scaling_factor = scaling_factor  # Scaling factor for F.softplus operation

    def forward(self, x):
        # torch.gt (greater than) operation
        x = torch.gt(x, self.threshold)
        # F.softplus operation
        x = F.softplus(x * self.scaling_factor)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vector_size: int, matrix_size: tuple):
        super().__init__()
        self.vector_size = vector_size
        self.matrix_size = matrix_size

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = x.view(-1, self.vector_size, 1)
        y = y.view(-1, 1, self.vector_size)
        result = torch.bmm(x, y)
        result = torch.round(result)
        return result

class Model(nn.Module):

        self,
        in_channels: int,
        pool_kernel_size: tuple,
        pool_stride: tuple,
        pool_padding: tuple,
        tanh_for_channels: bool,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.avg_pool = nn.AvgPool3d(kernel_size=pool_kernel_size, stride=pool_stride, padding=pool_padding)
        self.tanh = nn.Tanh() if tanh_for_channels else F.tanh

        x = self.avg_pool(x)
        x = self.tanh(x)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, cat_dim, reshape_shape):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_shape = input_shape
        self.cat_dim = cat_dim
        self.reshape_shape = reshape_shape

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        x1 = torch.reshape(x1, self.input_shape)
        x2 = torch.reshape(x2, self.input_shape)
        x = torch.cat((x1, x2), dim=self.cat_dim)
        x = torch.reshape(x, self.reshape_shape)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.linear1 = torch.nn.Linear(10, 5)
        self.linear2 = torch.nn.Linear(5, 1)

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply a linear transformation to the input tensor x
        x = self.linear1(x)
        # Apply another linear transformation
        x = self.linear2(x)
        # Use torch.where to apply a condition and choose between two tensors
        x = torch.where(x > 0, x, torch.tensor(0.0))
        # Use torch.bmm to perform batch matrix multiplication on the tensor
        x = torch.bmm(x.unsqueeze(2), x.unsqueeze(2).transpose(1, 2))
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, clip_value, exp_scale):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.clip_value = clip_value
        self.exp_scale = exp_scale

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = torch.exp(x * self.exp_scale)
        x = torch.clip(x, min=0.0, max=self.clip_value)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, split_size, split_dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.split_dim = split_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Split the input tensor along the specified dimension
        split_tensors = torch.split(x, split_size_or_sections=self.split_size, dim=self.split_dim)
        # Stack the split tensors along a new dimension
        stacked_tensor = torch.stack(split_tensors, dim=self.split_dim + 1)
        return stacked_tensor

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_groups: int, num_channels: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_channels = num_channels

        x = torch.neg(x)
        x = F.group_norm(x, self.num_groups)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, reduction_factor, alpha):
        super().__init__()
        self.leaky_relu = nn.LeakyReLU(alpha)
        self.reduction_factor = reduction_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.einsum("bixy->bxy", x)  # Example einsum operation

        x = torch.mean(x, dim=-1)
        x = x / self.reduction_factor
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, pad_width):
        super().__init__()
        self.pad_width = pad_width

    def forward(self, real, imag):
        real_padded = F.pad(real, self.pad_width)
        imag_padded = F.pad(imag, self.pad_width)
        complex_tensor = torch.stack((real_padded, imag_padded), dim=-1)
        complex_view = torch.view_as_complex(complex_tensor)
        return complex_view

        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.condition_number = condition_number

    def forward(self, x, condition):
        # Apply torch.logical_and to x and condition

        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, repeat_interleave_params, cos_params):
        super().__init__()
        self.repeat_interleave_params = repeat_interleave_params
        self.cos_params = cos_params

    def forward(self, x):
        repeat_interleave_params = self.repeat_interleave_params
        cos_params = self.cos_params
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, *repeat_interleave_params)
        x = torch.cos(x)
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, *cos_params)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = torch.log(torch.exp(x) + 1e-10)  # Apply log
        x = F.log_softmax(x, dim=1)  # Apply log_softmax
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.abs(x)  # Apply absolute
        x = x.view(-1, self.input_size)  # Flatten
        x = torch.argsort(x, dim=1)  # Sort along the second dimension
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, mid_layer_size, output_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear1 = nn.Linear(input_size, mid_layer_size)
        self.linear2 = nn.Linear(mid_layer_size, output_size)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.erf(x)
        x = F.softmax(x, dim=-1)
        x = self.linear1(x)
        x = torch.erf(x)
        x = F.softmax(x, dim=-1)
        x = self.linear2(x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_channels, num_groups):
        super().__init__()
        self.group_norm = nn.GroupNorm(num_groups=num_groups, num_channels=num_channels)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.group_norm(x, self.group_norm.num_groups)
        x = torch.maximum(x, torch.zeros_like(x))
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_sizes, output_size):
        super().__init__()
        layers = []
        previous_size = input_size
        for hidden_size in hidden_sizes:
            layers.append(nn.Linear(previous_size, hidden_size))
            layers.append(nn.ReLU())
            previous_size = hidden_size
        layers.append(nn.Linear(previous_size, output_size))
        self.sequential = nn.Sequential(*layers)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.sequential(x)
        x = torch.sign(x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, dilation, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv3d = nn.Conv3d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, dilation=dilation, bias=True
        )
        self.leaky_relu = nn.LeakyReLU(negative_slope=0.01)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv3d(x)
        x = self.leaky_relu(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, power=1.0, lambda_val=0.5):
        super().__init__()
        self.power = power

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.activation = nn.SiLU()
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = self.activation(x)
        x = F.hardtanh(x, min_val=-1, max_val=1)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, histc_bins, histc_min, histc_max):
        super().__init__()
        self.histc_bins = histc_bins
        self.histc_min = histc_min
        self.histc_max = histc_max

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.any(x, dim=0)
        x = torch.histc(x.to(torch.float), bins=self.histc_bins, min=self.histc_min, max=self.histc_max)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim: int, threshold: float, temperature: float):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold
        self.temperature = temperature

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = torch.ge(x, self.threshold)
        x = torch.softmax(x / self.temperature, dim=-1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, pad_width, pad_value, comparison_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.pad_width = pad_width
        self.pad_value = pad_value
        self.comparison_value = comparison_value

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x = F.pad(x, self.pad_width, value=self.pad_value)
        result = torch.eq(x, y)
        return result

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, clip_value, scale_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.clip_value = clip_value
        self.scale_factor = scale_factor

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clip(x, min=-self.clip_value, max=self.clip_value)
        x = F.interpolate(x, scale_factor=self.scale_factor)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, split_size, num_chunks):
        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.num_chunks = num_chunks
        self.hardswish = nn.Hardswish()

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=1)
        x = [self.hardswish(chunk) for chunk in x]
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, addcmul_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.sigmoid = nn.Sigmoid()
        self.addcmul_value = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(addcmul_value))
        self.input_size = input_size

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features1, in_features2, out_features):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear1 = nn.Linear(in_features1, out_features)
        self.linear2 = nn.Linear(in_features2, out_features)

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        x1 = torch.acos(torch.clamp(x1, -1.0 + 1e-7, 1.0 - 1e-7))
        x2 = self.linear2(x2)
        x = torch.einsum("bi,bj->bij", x1, x2)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, dilation=dilation, bias=False

        for param in self.bias.values():

        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, bias=False)
        self.relu = nn.LeakyReLU()
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv(x)
        x = self.relu(x)
        x = torch.sum(x, dim=1, keepdim=True)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Check if each element in the tensor is negative infinity
        isneginf = torch.isneginf(x)
        # Check if each element in the tensor is greater than 0
        gt = torch.gt(x, 0)
        # Combine the two operations using logical AND
        combined = torch.logical_and(isneginf, gt)
        return combined

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim)
        self.input_shape = input_shape

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x = F.embedding(x, self.embedding.weight)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings, embedding_dim, weight):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(
            num_embeddings,
            embedding_dim,
            padding_idx=None,
            max_norm=None,
            norm_type=2,

        )

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, width_factor, height_factor, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.width_factor = width_factor
        self.height_factor = height_factor
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        b, c, h, w = x.shape
        h_new = h * self.height_factor
        w_new = w * self.width_factor
        x = F.interpolate(x, size=(h_new, w_new), mode="bilinear", align_corners=False)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, min_value, custom_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.min_value = min_value
        self.custom_value = custom_value

    def forward(self, x):
        min_x = torch.min(x, dim=1, keepdim=True).values
        condition = min_x < self.min_value
        x = torch.where(condition, self.custom_value * x, x)
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, shift, dims):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.shift = shift
        self.dims = dims

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor1, tensor2, tensor3, alpha):
        super().__init__()
        # Initialize the tensors
        self.tensor1 = nn.Parameter(tensor1)
        self.tensor2 = nn.Parameter(tensor2)
        self.tensor3 = nn.Parameter(tensor3)
        self.alpha = alpha

    def forward(self, x):
        # Add two tensors
        x = torch.add(x, self.tensor1)
        # Add the product of two tensors with a scaling factor
        x = torch.addcmul(x, self.tensor2, self.tensor3, value=self.alpha)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.linear1 = nn.Linear(input_size, hidden_size)
        self.linear2 = nn.Linear(hidden_size, output_size)

import torch

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, reduction_ratio, epsilon):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.epsilon = epsilon
        self.reduction_ratio = reduction_ratio
        # Create arbitrary parameters based on the input size
        self.w = nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(input_size))
        self.b = nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(input_size // reduction_ratio))

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.min and torch.log operations in order
        min_val, _ = torch.min(x, dim=1, keepdim=True)
        log_val = torch.log(torch.clamp(x, min=1e-6))
        return torch.cat((min_val, log_val), dim=1)

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, start, end, steps):
        super().__init__()
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.steps = steps

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, tensor_shape1, tensor_shape2):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.tensor1 = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*tensor_shape1))
        self.tensor2 = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(*tensor_shape2))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.matmul(self.tensor1, self.tensor2)
        x = torch.count_nonzero(x)
        return x

import torch

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, kernel_size, dilation, padding, stride):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.kernel_size = kernel_size
        self.dilation = dilation
        self.padding = padding
        self.stride = stride

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.tril(x)
        x = F.unfold(x, kernel_size=self.kernel_size, dilation=self.dilation, padding=self.padding, stride=self.stride)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_height, input_width, num_groups, num_channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_height = input_height
        self.input_width = input_width
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_channels = num_channels

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply torch.tril to the last two dimensions of the input tensor
        x = x.transpose(-2, -1)  # Transpose to apply torch.tril to the last two dimensions
        x = torch.tril(x)
        x = x.transpose(-2, -1)  # Transpose back

        # Apply F.group_norm
        x = F.group_norm(x, self.num_groups)

        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, embedding_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        similarity = F.cosine_similarity(x1, x2, dim=1, eps=1e-8)

        return or_result

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, boundaries):
        super().__init__()
        self.boundaries = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(boundaries, dtype=torch.float32), requires_grad=False)

    def forward(self, x, y):
        x_bucketized = torch.bucketize(x, self.boundaries)
        y_bucketized = torch.bucketize(y, self.boundaries)
        result = torch.logical_and(x_bucketized == y_bucketized, x_bucketized > 0)
        return result

def get_init_inputs():
    return [input_shape, boundaries]

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, scaling_factor):
        super().__init__()
        self.scaling_factor = scaling_factor

    def forward(self, query, key, value):
        mask = torch.isneginf(query)
        attention_output = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(query, key, value, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False)
        attention_output = attention_output * mask
        attention_output = attention_output * self.scaling_factor
        return attention_output

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes, k, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.softmax = nn.Softmax(dim=dim)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Using torch.einsum to compute the sum along a specific dimension
        y = torch.einsum("nchw->nhwc", x)

        # Using nn.functional.interpolate to resize the tensor
        y = F.interpolate(y, scale_factor=2, mode="bilinear", align_corners=False)
        return y

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, num_heads, seq_length, head_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_size = batch_size
        self.num_heads = num_heads
        self.seq_length = seq_length
        self.head_dim = head_dim

    def forward(self, query, key, value):
        attention_weights = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(query, key, value)
        median = torch.median(attention_weights)
        return median

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_rows, num_cols, diagonal, clip_min, clip_max):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.diagonal = diagonal
        self.clip_min = clip_min
        self.clip_max = clip_max

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.tril(x, diagonal=self.diagonal)
        x = torch.clip(x, min=self.clip_min, max=self.clip_max)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, mid_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.silu = nn.SiLU()
        self.conv3d = nn.Conv3d(in_channels, mid_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding)
        self.conv3d_1 = nn.Conv3d(mid_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.silu(x)
        x = self.conv3d(x)
        x = self.silu(x)
        x = self.conv3d_1(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_channels, input_height, input_width, pad_size, pad_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.pad_size = pad_size
        self.pad_value = pad_value

        super().__init__()
        self.topk_k = topk_k

        super().__init__()
        self.clip_value = clip_value
        self.log2_base = log2_base

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clip(x, min=0.0, max=self.clip_value)
        x = torch.log2(x + 1e-9)  # Adding a small number to prevent log2(0)

import torch

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, hidden_dim, output_dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.fc = nn.Linear(input_dim, hidden_dim)
        self.elu = nn.ELU()
        self.output_layer = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, output_dim)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.mm(x, self.fc.weight.t()) + self.fc.bias
        x = self.elu(x)
        x = torch.mm(x, self.output_layer.weight.t()) + self.output_layer.bias
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, max_value, min_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_value = max_value
        self.min_value = min_value

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clip(x, min=self.min_value, max=self.max_value)
        mask = (x >= self.min_value) & (x <= self.max_value)
        x = torch.masked_select(x, mask)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_classes, diagonal=0):
        super().__init__()
        self.n_classes = n_classes
        self.diagonal = diagonal

    def forward(self, x):
        # Assuming the input tensor x is 2D with shape (batch_size, n_classes)
        # Convert the input to one-hot encoding
        x_one_hot = F.one_hot(x.long(), num_classes=self.n_classes)

        # Apply torch.triu to the 3D tensor obtained from one-hot encoding
        x_triu = torch.triu(x_one_hot, diagonal=self.diagonal)

        return x_triu

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels,
        out_channels,
        kernel_size,
        stride,
        padding,
        dilation,
        bias_shape,
        clamp_min,
        clamp_max,
        divisor,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min
        self.clamp_max = clamp_max
        self.divisor = divisor
        self.conv = nn.Conv2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, dilation=dilation, bias=False
        )
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv(x)
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.clamp_min, max=self.clamp_max)
        x = torch.remainder(x, self.divisor)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.pad = nn.ConstantPad1d(pad_size, pad_value)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=1)
        x = [self.pad(item) for item in x]
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=1)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, num_layers, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.num_layers = num_layers
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply acos in each element of the tensor
        x = torch.acos(x)
        # Stack tensors along a new dimension
        x = torch.stack([x] * self.num_layers, dim=self.dim)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings, embedding_dim, max_indices, min_indices):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim)
        self.max_indices = max_indices
        self.min_indices = min_indices

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.embedding(x, self.embedding.weight)
        max_values = torch.max(x, dim=1)[0]
        min_values = torch.min(x, dim=1)[0]
        condition = torch.where(max_values > self.max_indices, max_values, min_values)
        condition = torch.where(condition < self.min_indices, condition, max_values)
        return condition

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, hidden_dim, output_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.matmul = nn.Linear(input_dim, hidden_dim)
        self.silu = nn.SiLU()
        self.output = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, output_dim)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.matmul(x, self.matmul.weight.t()) + self.matmul.bias
        x = self.silu(x)
        x = torch.matmul(x, self.output.weight.t()) + self.output.bias
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.mask_value = mask_value

    def forward(self, x):

        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_features, glu_features):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.glu = nn.GLU(dim=1)
        self.input_features = input_features
        self.glu_features = glu_features

    def forward(self, x):
        batch_size, seq_len, _ = x.shape
        x = x.view(-1, self.input_features)

        x = torch.count_nonzero(x, dim=1)
        x = x.view(batch_size, seq_len)
        return x

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, num_group, num_channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.num_group = num_group
        self.num_channels = num_channels

    def forward(self, x):
        # Assuming 'isin_values' is a tensor that uses a subset of 'x'
        isin_values = torch.randn(self.input_size).to(x.device)
        mask = torch.isin(x, isin_values).float()

        # Apply group normalization
        x = F.group_norm(x, self.num_group)

        # Apply the mask to 'x'
        x = x * mask
        return x

        super().__init__()
        self.reduction_ratio = reduction_ratio

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, threshold: float):
        super().__init__()
        self.threshold = threshold

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Use torch.ge to create a mask of values greater than or equal to the threshold
        mask = torch.ge(x, self.threshold)
        # Use the mask to set values below the threshold to NaN
        x_nan = torch.where(mask, x, torch.tensor(float("nan")))
        # Use torch.nan_to_num to replace NaN with zero
        x_finite = torch.nan_to_num(x_nan, nan=0.0)
        return x_finite

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_embeddings, embedding_dim, use_embedding):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim)
        if use_embedding:
            self.embedding_layer = self.embedding
        else:
            self.embedding_layer = lambda x: x

    def forward(self, condition, x):
        x_embedded = self.embedding_layer(condition)
        x_masked = torch.where(x_embedded > 0.5, x, x * 0)
        return x_masked

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size

    def forward(self, x1: torch.Tensor, x2: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x1 = x1.view(-1, *self.input_size)
        x2 = x2.view(-1, *self.input_size)
        x = torch.logical_or(x1, x2)
        x = torch.minimum(x, x1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features: int, chunk_size: int, split_size: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.tanh = nn.Tanh()
        self.chunk_size = chunk_size
        self.split_size = split_size

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.tanh(x)
        x = torch.split(x, self.chunk_size, dim=1)
        x = [torch.split(chunk, self.split_size, dim=1) for chunk in x]
        x = [chunk[0] if idx % 2 == 0 else chunk[1] for idx, chunk in enumerate(x)]
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=1)
        return x

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, index_dim, mean_dim, index_tensor, mean_tensor):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.index_dim = index_dim
        self.index_tensor = torch.nn.Parameter(index_tensor, requires_grad=False)
        self.mean_dim = mean_dim
        self.mean_tensor = torch.nn.Parameter(mean_tensor, requires_grad=False)

    def forward(self, x):
        y = torch.index_select(x, self.index_dim, self.index_tensor)
        y = torch.mean(y, self.mean_dim)
        return y

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, split_size_or_sections, num_channels):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.split_size_or_sections = split_size_or_sections
        self.num_channels = num_channels

    def forward(self, x):
        # Apply ceil to the tensor
        x = torch.ceil(x)
        # Split the tensor into chunks
        x = torch.split(x, self.split_size_or_sections, dim=1)
        # Concatenate along the channel dimension
        x = torch.cat(x, dim=self.num_channels)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, repeat_times, maximum_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.repeat_times = repeat_times
        self.maximum_value = maximum_value

    def forward(self, x):
        # Repeat each element of input tensor for 'repeat_times'
        repeated_tensor = torch.repeat_interleave(x, repeats=self.repeat_times, dim=self.input_size)

        # Take the maximum between each tensor element and 'maximum_value'
        max_tensor = torch.maximum(repeated_tensor, torch.tensor(self.maximum_value))

        return max_tensor

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, condition_shape, values_shape, test_values):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.condition = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(condition_shape))
        self.values = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(values_shape))
        self.test_values = test_values

    def forward(self, x):
        condition = torch.where(x > self.test_values, self.condition, torch.tensor(0.0))
        result = torch.where(torch.isin(condition, self.values), condition, torch.tensor(0.0))
        return result

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, shift):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose1d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )
        self.shift = shift

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.roll(x, shifts=self.shift, dims=-1)
        x = F.conv_transpose1d(
            x,
            self.conv_transpose.weight,
            bias=self.conv_transpose.bias,
            stride=self.conv_transpose.stride,
            padding=self.conv_transpose.padding,
            output_padding=self.conv_transpose.output_padding,
        )
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, embedding_dim: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim

    def forward(self, x1: torch.Tensor, x2: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        similarity = F.cosine_similarity(x1, x2, dim=1, eps=1e-8)
        mask = torch.where(similarity > 0.8, torch.tensor(1.0), torch.tensor(0.0))
        return mask

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, multiplier, topk):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.multiplier = multiplier
        self.topk = topk

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.mul(x, self.multiplier)
        _, indices = torch.topk(x.view(x.size(0), -1), k=self.topk)
        x_max = torch.gather(x.view(x.size(0), -1), dim=1, index=indices)
        return x_max

        super().__init__()
        self.input_dim = input_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Assuming x is a 2D tensor, we will apply torch.logical_or on the first and second column

        super().__init__()
        self.pad_val = pad_val

        self.value = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(value_tensor_shape))
        self.exp_tensor = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(exp_tensor_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.where(x > 0, x, self.condition)
        x = torch.expm1(x)
        x = torch.where(x > 0, x, self.value)
        x = x + self.exp_tensor
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):

        pool_stride: tuple,
        pool_padding: tuple,
        pool_dilation: tuple,

        x = self.max_pool_3d(x)
        x = self.relu(x)
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, roll_shifts, max_dim):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        # Initialize weights
        self.input_size = input_size
        self.roll_shifts = roll_shifts
        self.max_dim = max_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        # Assume x has the shape (batch_size, input_size)
        x = torch.roll(x, shifts=self.roll_shifts, dims=self.max_dim)
        x = torch.max(x, dim=-1)[0]
        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride, padding, output_padding, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv_transpose = nn.ConvTranspose2d(
            in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=stride, padding=padding, output_padding=output_padding
        )

        x = x + self.bias

import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, target_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.input = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(input_size))
        self.target = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(target_size))

    def forward(self, input=None, target=None):
        if input is None:
            input = self.input
        if target is None:
            target = self.target
        distance = torch.dist(input, target)
        logits = F.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits(input, target)
        return distance, logits

        padding_idx,
        max_norm,
        norm_type,
        scale_grad_by_freq,
        sparse,
        clamp_min,
        clamp_max,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(
            num_embeddings,
            embedding_dim,
            padding_idx=padding_idx,
            max_norm=max_norm,
            norm_type=norm_type,
            scale_grad_by_freq=scale_grad_by_freq,
            sparse=sparse,
        )
        self.clamp_min = clamp_min
        self.clamp_max = clamp_max

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.embedding(x, self.embedding.weight)
        x = torch.clamp(x, min=self.clamp_min, max=self.clamp_max)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.dim = dim

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Apply torch.logsumexp along a specified dimension
        x = torch.logsumexp(x, dim=self.dim)
        # Find all non-zero indices in the tensor
        x = torch.nonzero(x)
        return x

import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, clip_value):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.gelu = nn.GELU()
        self.clip_value = clip_value

        x = self.linear(x)
        x = self.gelu(x)
        x = torch.clip(x, min=-self.clip_value, max=self.clip_value)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_channels, kernel_size, dilation, padding):
        super().__init__()
        self.kernel_size = kernel_size
        self.dilation = dilation
        self.padding = padding

    def forward(self, x1, x2):
        x1_unfolded = F.unfold(x1, kernel_size=self.kernel_size, dilation=self.dilation, padding=self.padding)
        x2_unfolded = F.unfold(x2, kernel_size=self.kernel_size, dilation=self.dilation, padding=self.padding)
        x = torch.minimum(x1_unfolded, x2_unfolded)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_shape, repeat_factor, min_axis):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_tensor = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(input_shape))
        self.repeat_factor = repeat_factor
        self.min_axis = min_axis

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.repeat_interleave(x, self.repeat_factor, dim=self.min_axis)
        x = torch.argmin(x, dim=self.min_axis)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, pad_width, pad_mode, median_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.pad_width = pad_width
        self.pad_mode = pad_mode
        self.median_dim = median_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.pad(x, self.pad_width, mode=self.pad_mode)
        x = torch.median(x, dim=self.median_dim).values
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, upsample_scale, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_norm = nn.BatchNorm2d(num_features)
        self.upsample = nn.Upsample(scale_factor=upsample_scale)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.batch_norm(x)
        x = self.upsample(x)
        x = x + self.bias
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_features, batch_size):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.tanh = nn.Tanh()
        self.batch_norm = nn.BatchNorm1d(input_features, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.tanh(x)
        x = x.view(-1, x.size(-1))  # Flatten the tensor before passing to BatchNorm1d
        x = self.batch_norm(x)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True):
        super().__init__()
        self.batch_norm = nn.BatchNorm2d(num_features, eps, momentum, affine, track_running_stats)
        self.softplus = nn.Softplus()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.batch_norm(x)
        x = self.softplus(x)
        return x

class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super(Model, self).__init__()

    def forward(self, x):

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size, output_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear1 = nn.Linear(input_size, hidden_size)
        self.tanh = nn.Tanh()
        self.linear2 = nn.Linear(hidden_size, output_size)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear1(x)
        x = self.tanh(x)
        x = self.linear2(x)
        x = torch.logsumexp(x, dim=1)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_sizes, output_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(input_size, hidden_sizes)
        self.hardshrink = nn.Hardshrink()
        self.linear_output = nn.Linear(hidden_sizes, output_size)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.sort(x, dim=1)[0]  # torch.srt
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = self.hardshrink(x)
        x = self.linear_output(x)
        return x
